subject: AP Physics 1
custom_notes: Reviewed Unit 2 Force and Translational Dynamics bank with answer-only formatting for import.

questionTypes.type: Identify properties of systems and calculate center of mass

questions.question: Which of the following properties is ignored when treating a complex system as a single point mass using the object model?
options: Velocity of the center of mass

questions.question: For a system with a symmetrical mass distribution, where is the center of mass located?
options: At the edge of the system
It cannot be determined without calculation

questions.question: A system consists of two 1 kg masses located at x = 0 m and x = 2 m. Where is the center of mass of this system?
What happens to the analysis of a system if the interactions between constituent objects are not important for modeling macroscopic behavior?
options: The system can be treated as a single object
questions.correct_option: The system can be treated as a single object
questions.answer: B. The system can be treated as a single object

questionTypes.type: Construct and interpret free-body diagrams (FBDs)

questions.question: An object hangs by a string from a car's rearview mirror. If the car is speeding up and moving to the right, which forces should appear on the FBD of the object?
options: Gravity, Tension, and a forward 'Force of Motion'

questions.question: Where should the arrows originate when drawing individual forces on a free-body diagram on the AP Physics exam?
options: From the surface of the object
From a single dot representing the object
From the center of the Earth
From the tip of the previous arrow
questions.correct_option: From a single dot representing the object
questions.answer: B. From a single dot representing the object

questions.question: A block is at rest on a rough incline. Which force points exactly opposite to the normal force in the FBD?
options: The component of gravity perpendicular to the surface
The component of gravity parallel to the surface
questions.correct_option: The component of gravity perpendicular to the surface
questions.answer: B. The component of gravity perpendicular to the surface

questions.question: When an object is in free fall, what force(s) should be shown on its free-body diagram if air resistance is negligible?

questionTypes.type: Identify Newton's Third Law interaction pairs

questions.question: A string pulls on a block with a tension force T. According to Newton's third law, what is the reaction force?
options: The block pulls on the string with force T
The normal force on the block
questions.correct_option: The block pulls on the string with force T
questions.answer: B. The block pulls on the string with force T

questions.question: Which of the following is a necessary condition for two forces to be a Newton's third law interaction pair?
options: They must act on the same object
They must act on two different objects
One must be a contact force and one must be a field force
questions.correct_option: They must act on two different objects
questions.answer: C. They must act on two different objects

questions.question: A student pushes down on a table. The interaction pair to this push is the:
options: Table pushing up on the student
Gravity pulling down on the table
Normal force from the floor on the student
questions.correct_option: Table pushing up on the student
questions.answer: B. Table pushing up on the student

questions.question: In a collision between a heavy truck and a light car, how do the magnitudes of the forces they exert on each other compare?
options: The truck exerts a greater force
The car exerts a greater force
They exert equal forces on each other
It depends on which vehicle is moving faster
questions.correct_option: They exert equal forces on each other
questions.answer: C. They exert equal forces on each other

questionTypes.type: Apply Newton's First Law to translational equilibrium

questions.question: If an object is moving at a constant velocity, what can be concluded about the net force acting on it?
options: It is equal to the object's mass

questions.question: A traffic light is suspended by two symmetric cables. If the net force on the light is zero, what must be true about the vertical components of the tensions?
options: They are each equal to the weight
Their sum equals the weight of the light
They are larger than the horizontal components
questions.correct_option: Their sum equals the weight of the light
questions.answer: B. Their sum equals the weight of the light

questions.question: In which type of reference frame would an observer verify Newton's first law of motion?
An object experiences balanced forces in the y-direction but unbalanced forces in the x-direction. Describe its motion.
options: Constant velocity in y; acceleration in x
The object must be at rest
questions.correct_option: Constant velocity in y; acceleration in x
questions.answer: B. Constant velocity in y; acceleration in x

questionTypes.type: Solve for acceleration using Newton's Second Law

questions.question: A net force of 10 N acts on a 2 kg object. What is the acceleration of the object?
If the net force on an object is directed to the right, what is the direction of the object's acceleration?
options: It depends on the velocity direction

questions.question: A drag racer speeds up from rest to 22 m/s in 2 s. If the racer's mass is 800 kg, what is the net force acting on the racer?
If you double the net force acting on an object while keeping its mass constant, how does the acceleration change?

questionTypes.type: Analyze multi-body system dynamics and connected objects

questions.question: Two blocks (2 kg and 3 kg) are connected by a string and pulled across a frictionless surface by a 10 N force. What is the acceleration of the system?
In the system from the previous question, what is the tension in the string connecting the two blocks (if the force pulls the 3 kg block)?
A 0.5 kg cart on a frictionless table is connected to a 0.1 kg hanging mass via a pulley. What is the net force on this two-object system?
As a hanging object in a two-body system falls and speeds up, how does the tension in the rope compare to the object's weight (mg)?

questionTypes.type: Compare inertial and gravitational mass

questions.question: Which type of mass determines an object's resistance to changes in its motion?
In an experiment, a student uses a steady 0.2 N force to accelerate a cart and uses a motion detector to find acceleration. What type of mass are they measuring?
Which of the following is related to the force of attraction between two systems with mass?
According to the principle of equivalence, how do inertial and gravitational mass compare?
options: They have been experimentally verified to be equivalent
questions.correct_option: They have been experimentally verified to be equivalent
questions.answer: C. They have been experimentally verified to be equivalent

questionTypes.type: Calculate gravitational force using the Universal Law

questions.question: If the distance between two masses is doubled, the gravitational force between them:
The gravitational force between two objects is F. If one mass is tripled and the other is doubled, what is the new force?
Newton's law of universal gravitation describes the force as being inversely proportional to the square of:
options: The distance between the centers of mass
questions.correct_option: The distance between the centers of mass
questions.answer: B. The distance between the centers of mass

questions.question: Which direction does the gravitational field point for a spherically symmetric mass?

questionTypes.type: Determine gravitational field strength 'g' in varying scenarios

questions.question: Planet Z has twice the mass of Earth and twice the diameter of Earth. How does the 'g' on Planet Z compare to Earth's 'g'?
The magnitude of the gravitational field on a planet's surface is 2g. If the planet's mass is half of Earth's mass, its radius is:
What happens to the gravitational field strength as you move twice as far from the center of a planet?
questions.answer: C. It is reduced to one-fourth

questions.question: An astronaut is on a planet with 3 times Earth's mass and 3 times Earth's radius. Their weight there is:

questionTypes.type: Calculate static friction and the threshold of motion

questions.question: The maximum force of static friction for a box is 25 N. If a horizontal force of 20 N is applied to the box at rest, what is the static friction force?
Which of the following determines the maximum value of static friction between two surfaces?
options: Normal force and coefficient of static friction
questions.correct_option: Normal force and coefficient of static friction
questions.answer: B. Normal force and coefficient of static friction

questions.question: A block is on a surface where mu_s = 0.5 and mu_k = 0.3. If the block is at rest and a force equal to the maximum static friction is applied, what happens?
In which direction is the static friction force exerted?
options: In the direction required to prevent slipping
questions.correct_option: In the direction required to prevent slipping
questions.answer: C. In the direction required to prevent slipping

questionTypes.type: Calculate kinetic friction in translational motion

questions.question: The force of kinetic friction between two surfaces does NOT depend on which of the following?
questions.answer: C. The surface area of contact

questions.question: A puck slides across a table. The kinetic friction force is exerted in which direction?
options: Opposite to the velocity relative to the table
Toward the center of the table
questions.correct_option: Opposite to the velocity relative to the table
questions.answer: A. Opposite to the velocity relative to the table

questions.question: If you double the mass of a sliding block, the kinetic friction force:
What is the primary macroscopic cause of contact forces like friction?

questionTypes.type: Analyze forces and motion on inclined planes

questions.question: For a block on a frictionless incline of angle theta, what is the acceleration down the ramp?
How does the normal force on a block change as the angle of an incline increases from 0 to 90 degrees?
A block of mass m is at rest on a rough incline. Which force has a magnitude equal to mg sin(theta)?
Two ramps have the same height but different angles. Which is true for a block sliding down either frictionless ramp from rest?
options: Ramp with larger angle gives higher final speed
Ramp with smaller angle gives higher final speed
They result in the same final speed
Acceleration is the same for both
questions.correct_option: They result in the same final speed
questions.answer: C. They result in the same final speed

questionTypes.type: Apply Hooke's Law to calculate spring force

questions.question: A force of 4 N compresses a spring by 0.05 m. What is the spring constant k?
The magnitude of the force exerted by an ideal spring is proportional to:
options: The change in its length from relaxed length
The time it has been stretched
questions.correct_option: The change in its length from relaxed length
questions.answer: B. The change in its length from relaxed length

questions.question: In which direction is the spring force always directed?
If you double the stretch of a spring, the restoring force:

questionTypes.type: Analyze circular motion and centripetal acceleration

questions.question: In uniform circular motion, which way does the centripetal acceleration vector point?
If an object's speed in a circular path is doubled while the radius is kept constant, the centripetal acceleration:
Which component of an object's acceleration is responsible for changing its direction of motion?
For an object traveling at constant speed in a circle, the period T is proportional to:

questionTypes.type: Analyze net force in circular motion (Centripetal Force)

questions.question: A ball is twirled in a vertical circle. At the very bottom, which force equation correctly represents the centripetal force?
A car travels over a circular hill. At the crest, the normal force on the car is:
What provides the centripetal force for a car turning on a level, unbanked road?
A pendulum bob is swinging. At the bottom of its 180-degree arc, it is neither speeding up nor slowing down. The force registered by a sensor there is:

questionTypes.type: Analyze non-inertial frames and apparent weight

questions.question: A man stands on a scale in an elevator accelerating upward. The scale reading will be:
A weightlifter lifting a barbell at constant speed suddenly slows to a stop. During this slowing interval, the scale reading will:
In a free-falling elevator, the apparent weight of an object is:
An object accelerates upward. From Newton's second law, the normal force must satisfy:

questionTypes.type: Analyze circular orbits and Kepler's Third Law

questions.question: For a satellite in circular orbit, which force provides the centripetal acceleration?
What happens to the speed of a satellite if it is moved to an orbit with a larger radius?
According to the derived orbital period equation, T^2 is proportional to:
options: The cube of the radius R^3
questions.correct_option: The cube of the radius R^3
questions.answer: C. The cube of the radius R^3

questions.question: To calculate the speed of a satellite in orbit, one must know the mass of:
options: Both the satellite and the central body
Neither, speed is independent of mass

questionTypes.type: Linearize dynamics data to determine physical constants

questions.question: To determine 'g' from a graph of height h versus time t for an object starting at rest, one should plot:
The slope of a graph of Force vs. Elongation for a spring represents:
A student plots weight (N) on the y-axis and mass (kg) on the x-axis. The slope of this graph is:
To find the coefficient of kinetic friction, a student should plot friction force vs:

questionTypes.type: Design experiments for translational dynamics

questions.question: Which tool is best for measuring the instantaneous acceleration of a cart in a dynamics lab?
To experimentally determine the coefficient of static friction, a student should find:
options: The maximum angle of an incline before slipping
The time to slide down a ramp
questions.correct_option: The maximum angle of an incline before slipping
questions.answer: B. The maximum angle of an incline before slipping

questions.question: When measuring the period of a rotating device, why should multiple trials be performed?
options: To reduce random error and uncertainty
To satisfy the AP Course Audit
questions.correct_option: To reduce random error and uncertainty
questions.answer: B. To reduce random error and uncertainty

questions.question: In a lab to determine spring constant k, which setup allows for the most direct measurement?
options: Hanging various known masses and measuring stretch
Timing oscillations of an unknown mass
Dropping the spring from a height
questions.correct_option: Hanging various known masses and measuring stretch
questions.answer: A. Hanging various known masses and measuring stretch

questionTypes.type: Predictive scaling and functional dependence in dynamics

questions.question: If you double the mass of a cart but apply the same net force, the acceleration will:
To quadruple the gravitational force between two objects, the distance between them must be:
A spring is stretched a distance x, producing restoring force F. To produce force 3F, the stretch must be:
If an object's speed in a circle is tripled, the required centripetal force increases by factor:
